pages.text:
Fixed Deposit SOPs
Page 1
Internal Reference – Customer Service
 Fixed Deposit – Standard Operating
 Procedures
 Servicing SOPs, turnaround times, and escalation triggers
 Document Code: FD-SOP-03  |  Version 1.0  |  June 2026
 Fixed Deposit Servicing Knowledge Base

Fixed Deposit SOPs
Page 2
Internal Reference – Customer Service
This document sets out the Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for the most common Fixed Deposit
(FD) servicing requests. Each SOP states the trigger, the documents required, the resolution steps, and
the turnaround time (TAT). These SOPs are written for use by customer service teams and by
automated systems that triage and route depositor emails.
1. Premature Withdrawal SOP
SOP 1: Premature Withdrawal
Trigger: Depositor requests closure of an active FD before its maturity date.
Documents required:
●
FD account number
●
Identity proof matching the FD holder
●
Cancelled cheque or bank statement for payout, if the registered bank account has changed
Resolution steps:
1
Verify the FD account number and confirm the deposit start date and current status.
2
Confirm the lock-in period (3 months) has been completed and the FD type permits premature
withdrawal (tax-saving FDs are excluded).
3
Calculate the applicable interest rate after the premature withdrawal penalty, based on the period
actually held.
4
Share the computed payout amount with the depositor for confirmation before processing.
5
On confirmation, initiate closure and credit the amount to the registered bank account.
Turnaround time (TAT): 2 to 3 working days after depositor confirmation.
2. Maturity Payout SOP
SOP 2: Maturity Payout
Trigger: FD reaches its maturity date and the depositor has indicated (or auto-renewal rules require) a
withdrawal of the matured amount.
Documents required:
●
FD account number
●
Updated bank account details, if different from the registered account
Resolution steps:
1
System generates a maturity reminder 3 working days before the maturity date.
2
Capture depositor instruction: full withdrawal, renewal, or split.
3
If withdrawal is selected, verify the payout bank account is active and KYC is current.
4
Process payout on the maturity date, or the next working day if maturity falls on a holiday.
5
Send a payout confirmation with the final amount and TDS deducted, if any.
Turnaround time (TAT): Same day as maturity, or within 2 working days if the bank account requires
verification.
Escalation: If payout has not been received 3 working days past maturity, treat as a payout-failure case and
route to the Interest/Payout Delay SOP below.

Fixed Deposit SOPs
Page 3
Internal Reference – Customer Service
3. Auto-Renewal Reversal SOP
SOP 3: Auto-Renewal Reversal
Trigger: Depositor did not intend for the FD to auto-renew and requests a reversal within the permitted
window.
Documents required:
●
FD account number
●
Date of auto-renewal
●
Withdrawal instruction
Resolution steps:
1
Confirm the auto-renewal date and check that the reversal request is within 7 calendar days of that
date.
2
Recalculate the deposit as a premature closure of the renewed term, applying the standard premature
withdrawal penalty for the (typically short) period held after renewal.
3
Share the recalculated amount with the depositor for confirmation.
4
Process the payout on confirmation.
Turnaround time (TAT): 2 to 3 working days after depositor confirmation.
Escalation: Requests received after the 7-day window cannot be processed as a reversal; they must be
handled as a standard premature withdrawal of the renewed deposit.
4. Interest / Maturity Payout Delay SOP
SOP 4: Interest or Maturity Payout Delay
Trigger: Depositor reports that a periodic interest payout or maturity amount due has not been received in
the registered bank account.
Documents required:
●
FD account number
●
Approximate due date of the missed payout
Resolution steps:
1
Verify in the system whether the payout was actually processed and on what date.
2
If the payout was processed but not received, check the bank account validity (IFSC, account status,
KYC hold) for a bounce or rejection.
3
If the payout was not processed, identify the cause (system exception, KYC hold, pending bank
account verification) and clear it.
4
Reprocess the payout and confirm credit with the depositor.
5
If funds were returned by the bank, correct the account details before reprocessing.
Turnaround time (TAT): 3 working days from when the case is logged, excluding cases pending fresh
KYC documents from the depositor.
5. KYC Update SOP

Fixed Deposit SOPs
Page 4
Internal Reference – Customer Service
SOP 5: KYC Detail Update
Trigger: Depositor requests an update to address, mobile number, email, or identity documents on file.
Documents required:
●
Proof relevant to the field being updated (e.g., Aadhaar/utility bill for address, PAN for name correction)
●
FD account number(s) to be updated
Resolution steps:
1
Verify the submitted proof against the requested change.
2
Update the depositor's KYC record in the core system.
3
Propagate the change across all linked FD accounts.
4
Send a confirmation of the updated details to the depositor.
Turnaround time (TAT): 1 working day for mobile number/email; up to 5 working days for address or
identity document changes requiring verification.
6. TDS Certificate (Form 16A) Issuance SOP
SOP 6: TDS Certificate Issuance
Trigger: Depositor requests Form 16A for a financial year, typically for income tax filing.
Documents required:
●
FD account number
●
Financial year for which the certificate is needed
Resolution steps:
1
Confirm that TDS was deducted for the depositor in the requested financial year.
2
Check whether the relevant quarter's TDS return has been filed; the certificate can only be issued after
this.
3
Generate Form 16A from the system and make it available on the depositor portal.
4
Share the certificate with the depositor by email if requested.
Turnaround time (TAT): Immediate, if the relevant TDS return is already filed; otherwise, within 5 working
days of the statutory due date for that quarter's TDS return.
7. No-Objection Certificate (NOC) Issuance SOP
This SOP applies primarily to loan accounts but is included here as FD-linked loans and overdrafts are
closed using the same process, and depositors frequently raise this request alongside FD queries.
SOP 7: NOC Issuance on Closure
Trigger: Depositor or borrower requests an NOC after fully closing a loan or an overdraft taken against an
FD.
Documents required:
●
Loan or overdraft account number
●
Proof of final payment, if not already reflected in the system
Resolution steps:
1
Confirm the account shows a zero outstanding balance as of the closure date.

Fixed Deposit SOPs
Page 5
Internal Reference – Customer Service
2
Verify there are no pending charges, penal interest, or returned-payment holds.
3
Generate the NOC confirming closure and no outstanding dues.
4
Dispatch the NOC to the depositor's registered email and make it available on the portal.
Turnaround time (TAT): 5 working days from confirmed closure.
Escalation: If closure shows on the depositor's end but not in the system (e.g., a cheque still being cleared),
hold the case and inform the depositor of the expected clearance date rather than rejecting the request
outright.
8. Nomination & Death Claim SOP
SOP 8: Death Claim Settlement
Trigger: A nominee or legal heir reports the death of an FD holder and requests settlement of the deposit.
Documents required:
●
Original death certificate
●
Original FD receipt
●
Identity proof of the claimant
●
Proof of relationship or succession (nomination form on file, legal heir certificate, or succession
certificate where there is no nomination)
●
Claim form
Resolution steps:
1
Verify the death certificate and match the depositor's details against the FD account.
2
Check whether a nominee is registered on the account.
3
If a nominee is registered, verify the nominee's identity and process the claim in their favour.
4
If no nominee is registered, request a legal heir or succession certificate before processing the claim.
5
Settle the claim either by payout to the claimant's bank account or by transfer of the FD into the
claimant's name, as elected.
Turnaround time (TAT): 10 working days from submission of complete documents; this case type is not
subject to the standard servicing TATs given the additional verification required.
Escalation: Handle all death claim communication with extra sensitivity and avoid repeated requests for the
same document; collect the full document list in the first response wherever possible.
9. Bank Account Change (for Interest / Maturity Payout) SOP
SOP 9: Bank Account Change
Trigger: Depositor's previously registered bank account is closed or changed, and a new account needs
to be linked for future payouts.
Documents required:
●
Cancelled cheque or bank statement of the new account
●
FD account number(s) to be updated
Resolution steps:

Fixed Deposit SOPs
Page 6
Internal Reference – Customer Service
1
Verify the new account belongs to the FD holder (name match).
2
Update the payout bank account on all linked FD accounts.
3
If a payout is due within 5 working days, flag the FD for manual confirmation before the scheduled
payout runs.
4
Confirm the update with the depositor.
Turnaround time (TAT): 2 working days; allow an additional 3 working days' buffer before a scheduled
payout to avoid the payout going to the old account.
10. Duplicate Statement / FD Receipt SOP
SOP 10: Duplicate Statement or FD Receipt Request
Trigger: Depositor requests a duplicate FD receipt, interest certificate, or account statement.
Documents required:
●
FD account number
●
Period or financial year for which the statement is required
Resolution steps:
1
Verify the FD account number and depositor identity.
2
Generate the requested statement or duplicate receipt from the system.
3
Share it through the depositor portal or by email, as preferred.
Turnaround time (TAT): 2 working days.
